seo.title: Building Research Agents with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with Semantic Kernel enables multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis powered by plugins, planners, and memory. These agents excel at gathering and summarizing information but are prone to cascading errors in plugin chaining, invalid plans, memory hallucinations, and data contamination. Vex integration as a kernel filter provides essential guardrails for source verification, claim accuracy, recency, and bias detection in medium-risk research workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically use Semantic Kernel's KernelFunction plugins for web search (e.g., DuckDuckGo, Tavily), content extraction, and analysis tools. The Planner (Handlebars or Sequential) orchestrates execution by generating plans from natural language goals, chaining plugins like search → scrape → summarize → fact-check. Semantic Memory stores conversation history and retrieved documents for context-aware responses, while embeddings enable semantic retrieval of relevant past research.

The flow begins with user query → Planner generates XML plan → Kernel executes functions sequentially → Results feed into memory → Final synthesis via LLM. Common patterns include function calling with streaming results and memory chat history for multi-turn research sessions. Vex integrates as a post-function result filter to validate outputs before they propagate through the chain.

This architecture shines for enterprise research but fails when planners create invalid tool sequences or memory recalls fabricated sources, making reliability guardrails essential.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Search plugin returns malformed URLs, causing scrape plugin to fail and corrupting downstream analysis
risks.mitigation: Vex filter validates function outputs before passing to next plugin in chain

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Handlebars planner creates nonsensical sequences like summarize→search instead of search→summarize
risks.mitigation: Vex plan validator checks execution graph before kernel invocation

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Semantic Memory retrieves fabricated or outdated research context from embeddings
risks.mitigation: Vex memory filter verifies recency and source authenticity of recalled items

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Search results from biased sources pollute analysis and synthesis across plugin boundaries
risks.mitigation: Vex data isolation ensures clean handoffs between search, scrape, and analysis plugins

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent with Planner
implementation.description: Core Semantic Kernel setup with Handlebars planner for research workflows
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.planners import HandlebarsPlanner

kernel = Kernel()

# Add search and analysis plugins
kernel.import_plugin_from_function(
    search_web, plugin_name="Search"
)

planner = HandlebarsPlanner(kernel)
research_plan = await planner.create_plan(
    "Research latest AI regulations in EU"
)

result = await kernel.invoke(research_plan)
print(result)

implementation.title: Memory-Enabled Multi-Turn Research
implementation.description: Persistent chat history with semantic memory for iterative research refinement
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory

memory = SemanticTextMemory(storage=kernel_memory)
chat_service = kernel.get_chat_service("gpt-4")

history = await memory.search_async(
    "research_collection",
    "AI regulations",
    limit=3,
    min_relevance_score=0.7
)

chat_history = ChatHistory()
for item in history:
    chat_history.add_user_message(item.text)

response = await chat_service.get_chat_message_content(
    chat_history
)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Kernel Function Filter (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as result filter to validate plugin outputs for research agents
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from semantic_kernel import KernelFunction

vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

@kernel.register_function("Research")
async def research_web(query: str) -> str:
    # Original plugin logic
    results = await search_api(query)
    
    # Vex verification
    verified = await vex.verify({
        "input": query,
        "output": results,
        "verifications": ["source_verification", "recency_check"]
    })
    
    if not verified.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked: {verified.issues}")
    
    return results

vexIntegration.title: Vex Plan Validator (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Pre-validate Handlebars plans before execution to prevent invalid chains
vexIntegration.code:
async def validate_research_plan(plan: Plan) -> bool:
    plan_json = plan.to_dict()
    
    verdict = await vex.verify({
        "type": "execution_plan",
        "plan": plan_json,
        "context": "research_agent",
        "verifications": ["plan_validity", "tool_compatibility"]
    })
    
    return verdict.valid

# Before kernel.invoke
if await validate_research_plan(research_plan):
    result = await kernel.invoke(research_plan)
else:
    print("Blocked invalid plan")
